headline: Microsoft to rent Texas data center dropped by Oracle and OpenAI, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Microsoft has agreed to rent a data center project in Texas that ‌was originally being developed for Oracle and ‌OpenAI, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the ​situation.  Microsoft struck an agreement ‌with developer Crusoe ⁠after both Oracle and OpenAI walked away from talks to occupy the site, according ⁠to the report.  A spokesperson ​for Microsoft said it ‌had nothing to share, while Oracle and Crusoe did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

A spokesperson ​for Microsoft said it ‌had nothing to share, while Oracle and Crusoe did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

headline: Inside Arm’s $71 million chip lab where its making its first ever CPU
summary: For more than 35 years, Arm has licensed its chip architecture and collected royalties on every processor made by customers like Apple, Nvidia, Google, Amazon, Intel and Microsoft. Now Arm is becoming their competitor, making physical silicon of its own for the first time, with Meta as the initial customer. CNBC got an exclusive first look at Arm's new AGI CPU that's "ruthlessly optimized" for running AI inference in data centers, and toured the $71 million Austin lab Arm built for its new CPU.

raw.headline: Inside Arm’s $71 million chip lab where its making its first ever CPU
raw.summary: For more than 35 years, Arm has licensed its chip architecture and collected royalties on every processor made by customers like Apple, Nvidia, Google, Amazon, Intel and Microsoft. Now Arm is becoming their competitor, making physical silicon of its own for the first time, with Meta as the initial customer. CNBC got an exclusive first look at Arm's new AGI CPU that's "ruthlessly optimized" for running AI inference in data centers, and toured the $71 million Austin lab Arm built for its new CPU.

summary: Microsoft is expanding its security ecosystem with new integrations across Microsoft Security Copilot and the Sentinel data lake. Partners including Commvault, Rubrik, and XBOW are tying their platforms into Microsoft tools to connect threat detection, automated remediation, and recovery. headline: Technology Dashboard 2025: U.S. remains leading country of origin for European patent applications as China makes gains
summary: Companies and inventors from around the world filed a record 201 974 patent applications last year at the European Patent Office (EPO), according to the EPO Technology Dashboard 2025 (formerly Patent Index) published today. The U.S. maintained its position as the top country of origin for European patent applications, followed by Germany, China, Japan and Republic of Korea, with China rising to third place after a strong increase (+9.7%) in filings. headline: Nudge Security extends its AI security leadership with AI agent discovery
